questions.q: Which one of the following Indian states has the largest number of members in its State Legislature?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Himachal Pradesh has the largest State Legislature among the given options — 68 seats. Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, and Meghalaya each have 60 seats. Bicameral states: Bihar, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: In the Indian Order of Precedence, who amongst the following comes first?

options.D: The Chief Justice of the High Court
options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Chairman of UPSC, Chief Election Commissioner, and CAG all hold position 9A in the Indian Order of Precedence — same level. Chief Justice of High Court is at position 9 (before 9A). Since all three options A, B, C are at the same level, none 'comes first' among them, hence E is correct. Official BPSC answer: A.

questions.q: Who amongst the following is a land record officer?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Patwari is the land record officer at Subdivision or tehsil level. He is a link between the village and the government, the lowest state functionary in the revenue collection system, and maintains the record of ownership and tilling.

questions.q: The expression 'Socialist' was introduced in the Preamble of the Indian Constitution by which amendment?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: 42nd CAA 1976 ('mini constitution') added three words — Socialist, Secular, and Integrity — to the Preamble. Changed 'sovereign democratic republic' to 'sovereign socialist secular democratic republic' and 'unity of the nation' to 'unity and integrity of the nation'. Also added Fundamental Duties (Art.51A), introduced Central Administrative Tribunal, made advice of Council of Ministers binding on President.

questions.q: Constitution Day of India is celebrated on

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Constitution Day (Samvidhan Diwas) is celebrated on 26th November. On 26th November 1949, the Constituent Assembly adopted the Constitution; it came into force on 26 January 1950. Ministry of Social Justice notified this day to promote constitutional values. 26 November 1949 is also mentioned in the Preamble.

questions.q: Vote on Account is meant for

options.A: Vote on the report of CAG
options.C: Appropriating funds pending the passing of the budget
options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Vote on Account: special provision by which government obtains Parliament's vote to withdraw money when budget for new financial year is not released or elections are underway with caretaker government. Normally valid for 2 months; during election year may be extended. Article 116 of the Constitution defines Vote on Account.

questions.q: In India, registration of political parties is done as per the provisions of the

options.B: Election Commission in its proceeding of meetings
options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Registration of political parties is done under Section 29A of the Representation of People Act, 1951. ECI conducts free and fair elections (Article 324).

questions.q: National Commission for Women was created by

options.B: A decision of the Union Cabinet
options.C: An order of the President of India
options.D: An act passed by the parliament
options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: National Commission for Women was created by the National Commission for Women Act, 1990 — a statutory body set up in January 1992. First Chairperson: Mrs. Jayanti Patnaik (31 January 1992). Current Chairperson: Ms. Rekha Sharma. Bihar State Women Commission Chairperson: Mrs. Ashwamedh Devi.

questions.q: In Which case did the Supreme Court of India adopt the doctrine of 'prospective overruling'?

options.A: Kehar Singh Vs Union of India
options.C: Golak Nath vs State of Punjab
options.D: Champakam Dorairajan vs State of Madras
options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Doctrine of 'prospective overruling' first invoked in India in Golak Nath Vs State of Punjab by CJI K. S. Rao. Originates from American Judicial System — decision applies only in future, no retrospective effect. Minerva Mills: safety to Constitution's fundamental framework — Basic Structure doctrine. Kehar Singh: President's power under Art.72 subject to judicial review. Champakam Dorairajan: Fundamental Rights superior over DPSP.

questions.q: The provision of amending the constitutional provision is given in

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Procedure of amendment is laid down in Part XX (Article 368). This procedure maintains the sanctity of the Constitution and checks arbitrary power of Parliament.

questions.q: Who was the chairman of the Provincial Constitution Committee of the Constituent Assembly?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Constituent Assembly committees: Union Power Committee — Jawaharlal Nehru; Union Constitution Committee — Jawaharlal Nehru; Provincial Constitution Committee — Sardar Patel; Drafting Committee — Dr. B.R. Ambedkar; Advisory Committee on FRs, minorities and tribes — Sardar Patel.

questions.q: When was the Indian Parliamentary Group established?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Indian Parliamentary Group is an autonomous body formed in 1949 in pursuance of a motion adopted by the Constituent Assembly on 16 August 1948. It acts as a link between Parliament of India and Parliaments of the world.

questions.q: Which provision(s) of the Constitution came into force on 26th November 1949?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Provisions relating to citizenship, elections, provisional Parliament, and temporary provisions came into force on November 26, 1949. The remaining provisions came into force on 26 January 1950.

questions.q: Who among the following worked as Finance Minister of India for the longest period?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: P. Chidambaram served as Union Minister of Finance four times — the longest period among all Finance Ministers of India.

questions.q: The ninth schedule was introduced in the constitution of India during the Prime Ministership of?

options.E: None of the above/more than one of the above

questions.explanation: 1st Constitutional Amendment 1951 added the 9th Schedule — list of state and central laws that cannot be challenged in courts. Added during the tenure of Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.q: The Supreme Commander of India's Defence Forces is the

options.A: Chief of Staff of the Indian Army
options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Supreme Commander of the Indian armed forces is the President. He/She is Supreme Commander of the Indian Navy, Army, and Air Force; appoints Chiefs of the three armed forces; can declare war or conclude peace, subject to Parliamentary approval.

questions.q: Which Article of the Indian Constitution prescribes Hindi in Devanagari script as the official language of the Union?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Article 343(1): Hindi in Devanagari script shall be the official language of the Union. Art.343(2): continued use of English for 15 years (up to 25 Jan 1965). Art.343(3): Parliament may provide by law for continued use of English after 25 Jan 1965. Section 3(3): both Hindi and English shall be used for resolutions, general orders, rules, and administrative papers.

questions.q: Which of the following describes India as a Secular State?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: After 42nd Amendment (1976), the word 'secular' was added to the Preamble. Articles 25-28 (Right to Freedom of Religion) also reflect the secular ethos. The primary description of India as a Secular State is in the Preamble.

questions.q: When did the community development program start in India?

options.E: None of the above/more than one of the above

questions.explanation: Community Development Programme launched on 2nd October 1952 — first development programme in India. Objectives: development of material and human resources in rural areas; enhancement of local leadership; improve living standards; catalyze change in mindset.

questions.q: The Constitution of India was adopted by the

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Constitution of India was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on 26th November 1949 and came into force on 26th January 1950 to commemorate the idea of Purna Swaraj (1929). 26th November is celebrated as Constitution Day.

questions.q: The inspiration for 'Liberty, Equality, and Fraternity was derived from

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The ideals of Liberty, Equality, and Fraternity in the Preamble were adopted under inspiration from the French Revolution.

questions.q: Under which schedule of the constitution of India, the transfer of tribal land to private parties for mining can be declared null and void?

options.E: None of the above/more than one of the above

questions.explanation: Fifth Schedule deals with Administration and Control of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes. SC in State of Andhra Pradesh case (1997) declared that transfer of tribal land to private parties for mining under the Fifth Schedule was null and void. Sixth Schedule: tribal areas of Assam, Tripura, Meghalaya, Mizoram.

questions.q: Under which Schedule of the Constitution of India can the transfer of tribal land to private parties for mining be declared null and void?
questions.explanation: Under the Fifth Schedule, transfer of tribal land to private parties for mining can be declared null and void (SC judgment in State of Andhra Pradesh case, 1997).

questions.q: Cow slaughter was banned by law in Bihar in which year?

options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: Bihar Preservation and Improvement of Animals Act, 1955 prohibits slaughter of cow and calf. Article 48 (Gandhian DPSP) directs state to ban slaughter of cows, calves, milch and draught cattle. SC in 1958 held that complete prohibition on slaughter of cattle infringed upon the Fundamental Right of butchers to practice their trade.

questions.q: Who among the following has served as the Governor of Bihar twice?

options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: Akhlaqur Rahman Kidwai served as Governor of Bihar twice: 1979-1985 and 1993-1998. Also served as Governor of West Bengal and Haryana. Member of Rajya Sabha. Honored with Padma Vibhushan. Zakir Hussain and Ram Nath Kovind served as Governors of Bihar and later became Presidents of India.

questions.q: Who was the first non-congress Chief Minister of Bihar?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Mahamaya Prasad Sinha was the 5th Chief Minister of Bihar (March 1967 to January 1968) — the first non-congress government in Bihar. Member of 'Jan Kranti Dal'. Elected to 6th Lok Sabha from Patna constituency in 1977.

questions.q: How many maximum ministers, according to the law, can be appointed to the 243-Member of Bihar Assembly?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: As per 91st Constitutional Amendment, maximum number of ministers capped at 15% of total assembly strength. For Bihar (243 seats): 15% = ~36 ministers.

questions.q: Which of the following rights have not been declared as parts of Article 21?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Right to Information (covered under RTI Act 2005) and Right to electricity have NOT been declared parts of Article 21. Right to Sleep has been declared a part of Article 21 by the Supreme Court. Hence D (both B and C) is correct.

questions.q: Which of the following Articles promotes the idea of equal justice and provides free legal aid to the poor?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Article 39A (added by 42nd Amendment 1976) promotes equal justice and provides free legal aid to the poor. Art.38: state to promote welfare by securing a social order. Art.39: certain principles of policy to be followed.

questions.q: Which of the following statements is correct about the term 'Equality' mentioned in the Preamble of the Constitution? I. It refers to the absence of any special privileges for any section of the society; II. The preamble embraces civic and economic equality; III. The Preamble embraces only Political equality.

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Statements I and II are correct. The Preamble embraces equality of status and of opportunity — covers civic and economic equality. Statement III is incorrect — it is NOT only political equality. Hence D (I and II correct) is the answer.

questions.q: Which among the following does not come under the Fundamental Duties of a citizen in India?

options.A: To protect and Improve the Natural Environment
options.B: To develop scientific temper, humanism, and spirit of inquiry and reform
options.C: To strive towards the abolition of untouchability
options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Striving towards abolition of untouchability is NOT a Fundamental Duty under Article 51A. Abolition of untouchability is a Fundamental Right under Article 17. Fundamental Duties include protecting natural environment (g) and developing scientific temper (h).

questions.q: Matters of Inter-state river water disputes come under which Article?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Article 262 provides for adjudication of inter-state water disputes.

questions.q: India has borrowed the concept of Public Interest Litigation from which country?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: India borrowed the concept of PIL from America (United States). PIL can be filed in SC under Art.32 or High Court under Art.226.

questions.q: The first Lokpal Bill was introduced in the Parliament in the year

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: The first Lokpal Bill was introduced in Parliament in 1968.

questions.q: Consider the following statement(s): (i) The provisions related to Citizenship are covered under Part II of the constitution (Article 5-11). (ii) Indian Citizenship Act 1955 is based upon two principles- Jus Sanguinis and Jus Soli. (iii) Jus Sanguinis is the Law of Blood and Jus Soli is the Law of Soil. Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: All three statements are correct: (i) Citizenship in Part II (Articles 5-11); (ii) Indian Citizenship Act 1955 based on Jus Sanguinis and Jus Soli; (iii) Jus Sanguinis = Law of Blood, Jus Soli = Law of Soil.

questions.q: Which of the following does not come under the state list? i. Public order; ii. Police; iii. Public Health and sanitation; iv. Taxes on mineral rights

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: All four — Public order, Police, Public Health and sanitation, and Taxes on mineral rights — are subjects in the State List. Hence E (None of the above — all are in State List) is correct.

questions.q: Provisions relating to the administration and control of scheduled areas and scheduled tribes come under which schedule?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Fifth Schedule deals with Administration and Control of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes. Sixth Schedule deals with administration of tribal areas in Assam, Tripura, Meghalaya, and Mizoram.

questions.q: Schedule-Subject matter: (i) 5th schedule — Land Reforms; (ii) 10th schedule — Anti-defection law; (iii) 6th schedule — Provision related to tribal areas of some states; (iv) 7th schedule — Division of Power between the Union and the States. Which is/are not correctly matched?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Only (i) is incorrectly matched. Fifth Schedule deals with Administration and Control of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes, NOT Land Reforms (Land Reforms are in the Ninth Schedule). Statements (ii), (iii), (iv) are all correctly matched.

questions.q: Which of the following fact(s) about Preamble is/are Incorrect?

options.A: The Preamble is neither a source of power to the legislature nor a prohibition upon the powers of the legislature.
options.B: It is justiciable, that is, its provisions are enforceable in courts of law.
options.C: The Preamble is a part of the Constitution.
options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Statement B is incorrect. The Preamble is NOT justiciable — its provisions are not enforceable in courts. It is a part of the Constitution (confirmed in Kesavananda Bharati case) but it is non-justiciable.

questions.q: India is often referred to as a 'REPUBLIC'. What does Republic mean?

options.A: People have the supreme right to make decisions on internal as well as external matters.
options.B: Citizens cannot be discriminated against on the grounds of caste, religion, and gender.
options.C: The head of the state is an elected person and not a hereditary position.
options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Republic means the head of the state is an elected person and not a hereditary position. In India, the President (head of state) is elected. Option A describes 'Sovereign'.

questions.q: Which of the following systems represents the Indian electoral system?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Indian electoral system uses First Past The Post (FPTP) for Lok Sabha and State Assembly elections AND is a Representative Democracy (indirect democracy). Both features apply, so D (more than one) is correct.

questions.q: Which of the following is incorrect about the 'Attorney General of India'?

options.A: He can appear for the Government of India in only the Supreme court
options.B: He must be well qualified to become the Judge of the Supreme court
options.C: K K Venugopal is the present Attorney General of India
options.D: He is the highest law officer in the country
options.E: None/more than one

questions.explanation: Both A and C are incorrect. A is incorrect: AG can appear for GoI not only in SC but also in any other court when the case is referred to him. C is incorrect: R Venkataramani (16th AG, 2022) is the present AG, not K K Venugopal. Options B and D are correct. Hence E (A and C are incorrect) is the answer.
